Thanks for contributing to Orion. Keep PRs small and focused.
See CONTRIBUTING.md. Every change should be traceable to a principle.
-->

<!-- What problem does this solve, and why now? -->

## Scope

<!-- What's included — and explicitly what's not. -->

<!-- e.g. Closes #42 -->

<!-- What you ran/verified. For AI boundaries, describe how guardrails were tested (not the model's prose). -->

<!-- If there's a UI change. -->

<!-- Anything intentionally deferred. -->

## MVP Alignment
<!-- See docs/roadmap/mvp.md -->

- [ ] Does this belong in the v0.1 "yes" list?
- [ ] If not, why are we building it now?
- [ ] Which Product Principle does this support?
- [ ] Which Engineering Principle does this support?

## Principles
<!-- Traceability to the constitution. See docs/principles/ -->

**Supports:**
- <!-- e.g. Engineering #5 (events as source of truth), Product #2 (surface decisions) -->

**Trade-offs:**
- <!-- e.g. accepts some added complexity to preserve replaceability -->

## Product checklist
<!-- The six tests from docs/principles/product.md -->

- [ ] **Attention** — helps the user spend attention better
- [ ] **Decision** — ends in a clearer decision, not more data
- [ ] **Clarity** — makes the next choice easier, not harder
- [ ] **Explainability** — Orion can say *why*
- [ ] **Calm** — success is the user needing Orion *less*, not more
- [ ] **Restraint** — worth the complexity it adds

## Contribution checklist

- [ ] Follows accepted [ADRs](../docs/adr/) and the [domain language](../docs/domain/README.md)
- [ ] Docs updated in this PR (a change isn't done without docs)
- [ ] New significant + durable decision? Added/updated an ADR
- [ ] If AI-assisted: a human has reviewed, understood, and validated this change
